extraction_notes: PDF is an English-only speech (45 pages); the Kannada version is a separate PDF (bccc-kannada-budget-speech-2026-27.pdf) on OpenCity.in but not extracted here.

pdfplumber extracted clean English text with only 1.16% (cid:NNN) tokens — almost all confined to short Kannada verse quotations from D.V.G.'s Manku(cid:415)mmana Kagga and a few Basavanna / Allama Prabhu lines. OCR (kan+eng) was also run as an audit pass; English content was consistent between the two.

Speech is structured as 91 numbered items organised under 16 section headings (Budget Overview, Revenue, Properties, Markets, Administration, Public Relations, Advertising, Land Acquisition, Urban Planning, Finance and Accounts, Public Works, Sanitation, Lakes, Health, Animal Husbandry, Horticulture, Urban Forestry, Social Welfare, Public Education).
Amounts quoted in crore (1 crore = 100 lakh) are converted to lakhs here for consistency with the rest of this codebase.

Some items are revenue targets (property tax, advertising, Premium FAR, B->A Khata conversion, development/plan fees) rather than expenditures; these are captured and tagged with category 'revenue' where relevant.

Items without a specific named scheme or quoted amount have been omitted per spec (e.g. general 'improve roads' statements). Only concrete named initiatives, identifiable schemes, or items with quoted monetary allocations are included.

Page numbers below refer to speech-PDF page numbers (1-45). The source PDF uses internal 'Page | NN' numbers (48-92) which are the paginated position within a larger compiled budget book; those are not used here.

BCCC = Bengaluru Central City Corporation (sometimes 'BCentCC'). The speech identifies 63 wards, 6 assembly constituencies (Shivajinagar, Shantinagar, C.V. Raman Nagar, Chamarajapete, Gandhinagar, Chickpete), ~78 sq km, ~22 lakh population.

projects.name: E-PID numbers and e-Khata online system
projects.description: Issue E-PID numbers to all properties in the Corporation and enable e-Khata issuance through a software interface; 84,629 applications received and 84,173 final e-Khatas issued so far.
projects.verbatim_quote: Through an online system, we have created E-PID numbers for properties within the Corporation's jurisdiction, allowing citizens to obtain e-Khatas through a software interface.

projects.name: 2% stamp duty levy on new Khata issuance
projects.description: Levy 2% tax on stamp duty for issuing a new Khata to bring apartments and large development projects into the tax net and raise property tax revenue.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have decided to levy a 2% tax on stamp duty for issuing new Khatas, which will increase the Corporation's revenue.

projects.name: B-Khata to A-Khata conversion (5% guidance-value charge)
projects.description: Convert eligible properties from B-Khata to A-Khata through systematic digitisation and verification; levy a charge of 5% on guidance value for this conversion.
projects.verbatim_quote: Through systematic digitization and verification, we have successfully converted eligible properties from B-Khata to A-Khata. We levy a charge of 5% on the guidance value for this conversion.

projects.name: Property tax enforcement: notice, seal and auction of defaulting properties
projects.description: Identify properties with incorrectly self-assessed or unpaid property tax, issue notices, seal and auction properties on continued default to recover dues, and expand tax collection.
projects.verbatim_quote: If taxes remain unpaid even after issuing notices, we will seal the properties and proceed with auction.

projects.name: Kiosk service centres at Revenue Officers' offices
projects.description: Open kiosk service centres in the offices of the Corporation's Revenue Officers so citizens can pay property tax and access other services independently.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will open kiosk service centers in the offices of the Corporation's Revenue Officers, allowing citizens to pay property tax and access other facilities on their own.

projects.name: New parking policy for non-tax revenue
projects.description: Formulate a new parking policy to increase non-tax revenue.
projects.verbatim_quote: To increase non-tax revenue, we will formulate a new parking policy.

projects.name: Scientific management of outdoor advertising and PPP commercialisation of Corporation properties
projects.description: A new advertisement policy to scientifically manage outdoor advertisements on skywalks, bus shelters and public properties, and to commercially utilise Corporation properties through public-private partnerships for non-tax revenue.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will also focus on scientific management of outdoor advertising and the commercial utilization of Corporation properties through public-private partnership.

projects.name: Rental recovery and lease re-possession (438 Corporation properties)
projects.description: Identify properties where lease conditions have been violated among the Corporation's 438 owned properties (220 currently leased) and bring them back under Corporation possession; target rental fee revenue of Rs.58 crore.
projects.verbatim_quote: Within the Bengaluru Central City Corporation's jurisdiction, we have 438 properties from which we expect rental income of ₹58 crores. Of these, 220 properties are currently on lease.

projects.name: Asset monetisation: 5-30 year commercial leasing of unused buildings
projects.description: Lease vacant Corporation buildings, shops and offices to private institutions for long terms (5-30 years) to convert unused assets into commercial shops/offices and generate stable income.
projects.verbatim_quote: To increase the Corporation's revenue, we will mobilize financial resources through asset monetization. We will lease vacant Corporation buildings, shops, and offices to private institutions.

projects.name: Property record digitisation and fencing programme
projects.description: Manage property records systematically, collect property tax in full, and protect properties by installing fencing around them.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will also protect properties by installing fencing around them.

projects.name: Markets redevelopment campaign (112 markets, 4,772 shops)
projects.description: Revitalise historic markets including K.R. Market, Russell Market, Johnson Market and Kalasipalya Market while preserving their local character and providing modern amenities. Expected rental income of Rs.25 crore.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will revitalize historic areas such as K.R. Market, Russell Market, Johnson Market, and Kalasipalya Market – all located in the heart of Bengaluru – by preserving their local character while providing modern amenities.

projects.name: Russell Market comprehensive redevelopment (Shivajinagar)
projects.description: Comprehensive redevelopment of the century-old Russell Market in Shivajinagar with parking facilities and heritage structure preservation.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will spend ₹70 crores on the comprehensive redevelopment of Russell Market in Shivajinagar, ... a market with over a century of history. We will prioritize proper parking facilities and heritage structure preservation.

projects.name: Development of remaining markets
projects.description: Develop remaining markets under the Rs.90 crore markets-development allocation (Rs.70 crore for Russell Market, Rs.20 crore for other markets).
projects.verbatim_quote: We will develop the remaining market areas at a cost of ₹20 crores.

projects.name: Outsourced personnel deployment
projects.description: Deploy outsourced personnel as needed to streamline the administrative system (current strength: 1,164 officers/staff, 2,621 permanent sanitation workers, 843 direct-payroll sanitation workers, 410 contract staff).
projects.verbatim_quote: To further streamline the administrative system, we will deploy outsourced personnel as needed.

projects.name: Revenue-staff training on Total Station and GPS Survey
projects.description: Train Revenue Department staff on Total Station Survey and GPS Survey; hybrid-mode training for officers on GBG Act 2024, KMC Act 1976, KCSR 1966, RTI Act and Government Office Manual.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will train Revenue Department staff on Total Station Survey and GPS Survey. We will organize training workshops on new technologies to enhance the skills of technical officers.

projects.name: e-Office paperless administration
projects.description: Strictly implement the e-Office system and digitisation to bring transparency to administration and make office work completely paperless.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will strictly implement the e-Office system and digitization to bring transparency to administration and ensure that office work is completed quickly and in a completely paperless manner.

projects.name: BCCC official website and social-media platforms
projects.description: Develop the Corporation's official website and use official social-media platforms (along with print and electronic media) to continuously create awareness about services.
projects.verbatim_quote: To continuously create awareness among citizens about the Corporation's services, we are developing the Corporation's official website. Along with print and electronic media, we are effectively using the Corporation's official social media platforms.

projects.name: Modernisation of e-information dissemination and citizen outreach
projects.description: Modernise the e-information dissemination system using digital facilities and citizen outreach so residents receive information about and benefit from various schemes.
projects.verbatim_quote: In 2026-27, the Corporation is prepared to further modernize the e-information dissemination system.

projects.name: 24/7 Control Room and phone-in citizen outreach programmes
projects.description: Run a 24/7 Control Room to register public complaints and forward information to officers; organise phone-in citizen outreach programmes for direct communication with the public.
projects.verbatim_quote: The Control Room operates 24/7 to register complaints from the public and forward information to the concerned officers. In addition, we organize phone-in programs for direct communication with the public.

projects.name: Outdoor-advertising permit and renewal fees
projects.description: Levy annual ground rent and advertising fees on pedestrian skywalks, public toilets, underpasses and bus shelters constructed under PPP; target Rs.50 crore in permit and renewal fees.
projects.verbatim_quote: We estimate revenue of ₹50.00 crores from permit and renewal fees for advertising agencies.

projects.name: LED and digital screen advertising tenders under GBA Advertising Rules 2025
projects.description: Under the new Greater Bengaluru Area Advertising Rules 2025 (effective 13 January 2026), invite tenders to grant authorised advertising rights for LED and digital screens; expected revenue Rs.142 crore. Total Advertising Division revenue target: Rs.192 crore.
projects.verbatim_quote: In accordance with the new Greater Bengaluru Area Advertising Rules 2025, which came into effect on January 13, 2026, we have invited tenders to grant authorized advertising rights for LED and digital screens, bringing transparency to the system. Through this, we expect revenue of ₹142.00 crores.

projects.name: Land acquisition compensation (Kadiriyanapalya, C.V. Raman Nagar)
projects.description: Provide swift compensation to owners of land acquired in Kadiriyanapalya (C.V. Raman Nagar assembly constituency) and other pending acquisition cases via cash or Development Rights Certificates (DRC/TDR).
projects.verbatim_quote: we have set aside ₹50 crores for the current year to provide swift compensation to owners of land acquired in Kadiriyanapalya, which falls under the C.V. Raman Nagar assembly constituency, as well as for other remaining land acquisition cases within the Corporation's jurisdiction.

projects.name: Premium FAR policy (Section 18-B, KTCP Act 1961)
projects.description: Implement the Premium FAR policy via amendment under Section 18-B of the KTCP Act 1961, allowing collection of an additional charge of 0.4 times base FAR based on road width; expected additional revenue Rs.350 crore to be utilised for citywide development works.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have implemented the Premium FAR policy by introducing an amendment under Section 18-B of the KTCP Act 1961. ... Through this, we expect to generate an additional revenue of ₹350 crores.

projects.name: BCCC contribution to Greater Bengaluru Master Plan
projects.description: BCCC share of funding for the new Greater Bengaluru Master Plan being prepared by the Greater Bengaluru Authority; target draft completion in 11 months from April work order.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have set aside an allocation of ₹5 crores from the Corporation for this purpose.

projects.name: Padarayanapura Main Road widening (Chamarajapete)
projects.description: Widen Padarayanapura Main Road in Chamarajapete from 9 metres to 24 metres as part of the Master Plan work.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will also take up the widening of Padarayanapura Main Road in the Chamarajapete area from 9 meters to 24 meters.

projects.name: Business Corridor development on selected roads
projects.description: Develop selected roads as Business Corridors with state-of-the-art infrastructure; collect higher premium fees and reinvest in sustainable urban growth.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will develop selected roads as Business Corridors. We will provide state-of-the-art infrastructure in these corridors and collect higher premium fees.

projects.name: Nambike Nakshe scheme (same-day building plan approval)
projects.description: Under Nambike Nakshe, for sites up to 50x80, up to four residential units can be approved on a temporary basis through private architects/engineers with same-day online building plan approval.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Nambike Nakshe Scheme, for sites measuring up to 50x80, the construction of up to four residential units can be approved on a temporary basis through private architects or engineers.

projects.name: Occupancy Certificate exemption (30x40 G+2 / Stilt+3)
projects.description: Under Government of Karnataka order of 9 September 2025, residential buildings on 30x40 sites with G+2 or Stilt+3 are exempt from mandatory Occupancy Certificate requirement.
projects.verbatim_quote: residential buildings constructed on sites measuring 30x40 (1200 square feet) with 'Ground + 2 floors' or 'Stilt (Parking) + 3 floors' have been granted exemption from the mandatory requirement of obtaining an Occupancy Certificate (OC).

projects.name: Simplified building setbacks and ground-floor regulations
projects.description: Simplify building setbacks and relax open-space requirements; road-width-based allowance for additional residential units; stilt floor excluded from total building-height calculation.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have simplified building setbacks and relaxed the rules regarding the open spaces required around buildings, providing greater convenience to the public.

projects.name: Integrated Financial Management System (IFMS)
projects.description: Manage all payments, receipts and inter-office fund transfers of BCCC through the Integrated Financial Management System on a daily basis.
projects.verbatim_quote: the department manages all its payments through the Integrated Financial Management System (IFMS). Every financial transaction, including all receipts, payments, and inter-office fund transfers within the Corporation, will be systematically recorded in this system on a daily basis.

projects.name: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) system for welfare programmes
projects.description: Deliver all welfare programmes directly to beneficiaries through DBT under separate funding allocations; open a separate Personal Deposit Account for rapid NDRF/SDRF disbursement.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will deliver all welfare programs undertaken by the Bengaluru Central City Corporation directly to beneficiaries through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) system under separate funding allocations.

projects.name: Dedicated Internal Audit Cell for BBMP-era audit paragraphs
projects.description: Establish a dedicated internal audit cell to resolve pending audit paragraphs from BBMP days, objections/recovery notices from CAG and Chief Auditor reports, and internal BCCC audit items.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have established a dedicated Audit Cell with the aim of resolving pending audit paragraphs that have remained unsettled since the days of the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike.

projects.name: Public Works total budget (FY 2026-27)
projects.description: Total Rs.1,760 crore allocation for Corporation's public works — grants from Central/State governments plus Rs.200 crore raised through municipal bonds.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have allocated a total of ₹1,760 crores for the Corporation's public works. This budget comprises grants from the Central and State Governments, alongside ₹200 crores raised successfully through municipal bonds.

projects.name: Arterial / sub-arterial road asphalting (Special Infrastructure Capital Support Scheme)
projects.description: State grant of Rs.217.50 crore under the Special Infrastructure Capital Support Scheme to complete asphalting of 145 km of arterial and sub-arterial roads by May 2026.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Special Infrastructure Capital Support Scheme, the State Government has provided a generous grant of ₹217.50 crores to develop arterial and sub-arterial roads. We will complete the asphalting of 145 kilometers of these roads by May 2026.

projects.name: CM's Infrastructure Development Scheme: 115 km ward-road asphalting + 10 community buildings
projects.description: Rs.200 crore grant under the Honorable Chief Minister's Infrastructure Development Scheme to asphalt 115 km of ward roads and construct 10 new community buildings.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have secured a grant of ₹200 crores under the Honorable Chief Minister's Infrastructure Development Scheme. In the upcoming financial year, we will utilize this to asphalt 115 kilometers of ward roads and construct 10 new community buildings.

projects.name: CM's Special Grant to Chamarajapete / Gandhinagar / Shivajinagar
projects.description: Rs.75 crore under Chief Minister's Special Grant, allocating Rs.25 crore each to Chamarajapete, Gandhinagar and Shivajinagar assembly constituencies for critical local development works.
projects.verbatim_quote: under the Chief Minister's Special Grant, we have received ₹75 crores, allocating ₹25 crores each to the Chamarajapete, Gandhinagar, and Shivajinagar assembly constituencies.

projects.name: 10.96 km major stormwater drains (final stages)
projects.description: Final-stage development of 10.96 km of major stormwater drains (8.11 km already completed).
projects.verbatim_quote: we are in the final stages of developing 10.96 kilometers of major stormwater drains at a cost of ₹69 crores, with 8.11 kilometers already completed.

projects.name: World Bank-assisted Water Security and Disaster Management Program (stormwater upgrade)
projects.description: Upgrade stormwater drains to global standards under the World Bank-assisted Water Security and Disaster Management Program, to be implemented on full scale in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have formulated a comprehensive program to upgrade our stormwater drains to global standards under the World Bank-assisted Water Security and Disaster Management Program.

projects.name: Ward Development and Maintenance Fund (63 wards @ Rs.2.25 crore)
projects.description: Rs.141.75 crore for ward-level holistic development (Rs.2.25 crore per ward): Rs.1.5 crore per ward for development programmes (Rs.94.5 crore) and Rs.75 lakh per ward for maintenance works like filling potholes and desilting drains (Rs.47.25 crore).
projects.verbatim_quote: For the holistic development of our wards, we have set aside ₹141.75 crores, allocating ₹2.25 crores to each ward.

projects.name: Brand Bengaluru – Vibrant Bengaluru: Russell Market revitalisation + decorative electrification
projects.description: Rs.70 crore for historic revitalisation of Russell Market and Rs.15 crore for decorative electrification along the Corporation's major roads, under Brand Bengaluru – Vibrant Bengaluru.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Brand Bengaluru – Vibrant Bengaluru initiative, we have dedicated ₹70 crores in the budget for the historic revitalization of Russell Market, and ₹15 crores to install decorative electrification along the Corporation's major roads.

projects.name: 'Suraksha' scheme: 12 major junctions scientific upgrade
projects.description: Scientifically upgrade 12 major junctions under the 'Suraksha' scheme to streamline urban traffic.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will scientifically upgrade 12 major junctions under the 'Suraksha' scheme.

projects.name: 'Hejje Hejjege Surakshe' (Safety at Every Step) footpaths scheme
projects.description: Improve 50 km of existing footpaths and construct 15 km of new rainwater-absorbing, elderly-friendly, disabled-friendly footpaths; includes High-Rise Pedestrian Crossings (HRPCs) per Supreme Court directives. Allocation Rs.50 crore covers both Suraksha and Hejje Hejjege Surakshe.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have launched an innovative scheme called 'Hejje Hejjege Surakshe' (Safety at Every Step). Under this, we will improve 50 kilometers of existing footpaths and construct 15 kilometers of new, modern footpaths.

projects.name: 9 new pedestrian skywalks
projects.description: Invite tenders to construct 9 new pedestrian skywalks (24 skywalks currently operational), responding to traffic police requests.
projects.verbatim_quote: Currently, 24 pedestrian skywalks are operational. Responding to the traffic police's requests, we have invited tenders to construct 9 new skywalks.

projects.name: PPP bus shelters and free public toilets
projects.description: Build new bus shelters under PPP; leverage advertising rights to construct and maintain free public toilets in high-need areas citywide.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will also build new bus shelters under the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model. In 2026-27, we will leverage advertising rights to construct and meticulously maintain free public toilets in high-need areas across the city.

projects.name: Road microsurfacing programme
projects.description: Complete remaining 5.50 km of microsurfacing by April 2026 (25 km already completed) to strengthen road surfaces.
projects.verbatim_quote: To strengthen our road surfaces, we launched a Microsurfacing scheme and have already completed 25 kilometers. We will finish the remaining 5.50 kilometers by April 2026.

projects.name: White-topping of ~30 km major corridors
projects.description: Accelerate white-topping works on major corridors, targeting ~30 km by end of December 2026 for long-term durability.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have accelerated white-topping works, aiming to complete approximately 30 kilometers by the end of December 2026.

projects.name: Jet patcher machines and cold-asphalt technology
projects.description: Deploy jet patcher machines and cold-asphalt technology to rapidly address road wear in all weather conditions.
projects.verbatim_quote: we are deploying jet patcher machines and cold asphalt technology.

projects.name: Mechanical sweeping machines (7-year contract)
projects.description: Contracted 7 state-of-the-art mechanical sweeping machines for 7 years to clean busy roads at night; budget secured to expand to other major roads in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have contracted 7 state-of-the-art mechanical sweeping machines for a 7-year period to clean busy roads at night.

projects.name: Major building construction and renovation (schools, Revenue Officer building, stadium, dialysis, hospitals)
projects.description: Rs.60 crore for building projects: school construction/repair in Ward 117 (Shantinagar/C.V. Raman Nagar); new Revenue Officer building in Jeevan Bheemanagar; Sri R. Gundu Rao Stadium, kindergarten, gymnasiums and Seshadripuram swimming pool upgrade in Gandhinagar; Corporation Girls' High School Phase-2 in Rayapuram (Chamarajapete/Chickpete); dialysis centre, multipurpose community hall, children's hospital on Siddaiah Road; Kanakanayaka Bhavana in Jayanagar 2nd Block; new market building in Chinnayyanapalya.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have set aside ₹60 crores for major building construction and renovation projects to upgrade civic infrastructure.

projects.name: Sri R. Gundu Rao Stadium (Gandhinagar)
projects.description: Construct Sri R. Gundu Rao Stadium in Gandhinagar (part of the Rs.60 crore major building construction package).
projects.verbatim_quote: In Gandhinagar, we will construct the Sri R. Gundu Rao Stadium, a kindergarten, and gymnasiums, alongside upgrading the Seshadripuram swimming pool.

projects.name: Seshadripuram swimming pool upgrade
projects.description: Upgrade the Seshadripuram swimming pool under the building-construction/renovation package.
projects.verbatim_quote: In Gandhinagar, we will construct the Sri R. Gundu Rao Stadium, a kindergarten, and gymnasiums, alongside upgrading the Seshadripuram swimming pool.

projects.name: Corporation Girls' High School Phase 2 (Rayapuram, Chamarajapete/Chickpete)
projects.description: Launch Phase 2 development of the Corporation Girls' High School in Rayapuram as part of the Rs.60 crore building package.
projects.verbatim_quote: In Chamarajapete and Chickpete, we will launch Phase 2 development of the Corporation Girls' High School in Rayapuram.

projects.name: Children's hospital on Siddaiah Road
projects.description: Construct a children's hospital on Siddaiah Road.
projects.verbatim_quote: construct a dialysis center, a multipurpose community hall, a children's hospital on Siddaiah Road

projects.name: Kanakanayaka Bhavana (Jayanagar 2nd Block)
projects.description: Construct Kanakanayaka Bhavana in Jayanagar 2nd Block.
projects.verbatim_quote: the Kanakanayaka Bhavana in Jayanagar 2nd Block

projects.name: New market building in Chinnayyanapalya
projects.description: Construct a new market building in Chinnayyanapalya.
projects.verbatim_quote: and a new market building in Chinnayyanapalya.

projects.name: Flyover-underside micro-parks (PPP/CSR)
projects.description: Transform neglected spaces beneath flyovers into micro-parks, small business zones and artistic public hubs through PPP and CSR collaborations.
projects.verbatim_quote: Through PPP and CSR collaborations, we will transform neglected spaces beneath flyovers into vibrant micro-parks, small business zones, and artistic public hubs.

projects.name: Carbon Credit scheme: EV charging, bicycle tracks, walkways
projects.description: Under Carbon Credit scheme, promote pay-and-use EV charging points, construct dedicated bicycle tracks, develop pedestrian-friendly walkways, encourage community solar in commercial areas, mandate EV charging in all apartment complexes.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Carbon Credit scheme, our ultimate goal is a clean, green, and sustainable city. We will promote pay-and-use EV charging points, construct dedicated bicycle tracks, and develop pedestrian-friendly walkways.

projects.name: Urban Design Cell
projects.description: Establish a dedicated Urban Design Cell (Urban Transport Expert, Urban Designer, Urban Planner) — a first for BCCC — to reshape road, public space and traffic system design.
projects.verbatim_quote: To revolutionize urban planning, we have established a dedicated Urban Design Cell for the first time, comprising an Urban Transport Expert, an Urban Designer, and an Urban Planner.

projects.name: Solar panels and rainwater harvesting on Corporation buildings/lands
projects.description: Conduct strict energy audits of Corporation buildings and install solar panels for electricity self-reliance; construct rainwater harvesting pits in playgrounds, parks and vacant Corporation lands.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will conduct strict energy audits of Corporation buildings and install solar panels to achieve electricity self-reliance.

projects.name: Blue-Green Corridors (bioswales + tree planting)
projects.description: Construct Blue-Green Corridors on selected roads, featuring bioswales and extensive tree planting.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have allocated ₹10.00 crores to construct Blue-Green Corridors on selected roads, featuring bioswales and extensive tree planting.

projects.name: LED streetlight conversion (67,500 lights) + Centralized Control & Monitoring System
projects.description: Convert 67,500 traditional streetlights to energy-efficient LED lights integrated into a Centralized Control and Monitoring System (CCMS); 51,037 already converted. Expected ~60% reduction in BESCOM electricity bills.
projects.verbatim_quote: We are aggressively converting 67,500 traditional streetlights into energy-efficient LED lights, integrating them into a Centralized Control and Monitoring System (CCMS). We have already successfully converted 51,037 lights.

projects.name: Pourakarmika regularisation, biometric attendance, ID cards and health insurance
projects.description: Regularise eligible sanitation workers per government rules; facial and biometric attendance devices distributed; next phase: official ID cards and comprehensive health insurance for all 3,464 sanitation workers.
projects.verbatim_quote: We are actively regularizing eligible workers as per government rules. We have distributed facial and biometric attendance devices, and in the immediate next phase, we will issue official identity cards and provide comprehensive health insurance.

projects.name: Litter pickers, manual sweepers, compact mechanical sweepers + heavy-duty sweeping
projects.description: Procure litter picker machines and manually operated sweeping units for major roads, plus compact mechanical sweepers for dense market areas; Rs.10 crore for heavy-duty mechanical sweeping machines on contract.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have also allocated ₹10 crores to deploy heavy-duty mechanical sweeping machines on a contract basis.

projects.name: 20 SHE Toilets + 60 new public toilets
projects.description: Construct 20 dedicated SHE Toilets and 60 new public toilets with separate facilities for men, women and the disabled; systematic repair/upgrade of dilapidated older toilets.
projects.verbatim_quote: Prioritizing women's convenience, we will construct 20 dedicated SHE TOILETS and 60 new public toilets featuring separate facilities for men, women, and the disabled.

projects.name: Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Company (BSWML) allocation
projects.description: Rs.215 crore to BSWML for seamless waste collection, scientific segregation, landfill development and robust operation of solid waste processing plants.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have allocated ₹215.00 crores to the Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Company to ensure seamless waste collection, scientific segregation, landfill development, and the robust operation of solid waste processing plants.

projects.name: Mustering Centers and Suvidha Centers in every ward
projects.description: Establish well-equipped Mustering Centers and Suvidha Centers in every ward to provide sanitation staff essential resting infrastructure and amenities.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will establish well-equipped Mustering Centers and Suvidha Centers in every ward, providing them with essential resting infrastructure and amenities.

projects.name: Lake rejuvenation: Kaggadasapura, Ulsoor, Kelagina Byrasandra, Chickpete Byrasandra
projects.description: Conservation works underway on four lakes (boundary wire fencing, Home Guard deployment, tree planting); Rs.43.14 crore of development works already underway.
projects.verbatim_quote: We are prioritizing the conservation of Kaggadasapura, Ulsoor, Kelagina Byrasandra, and Chickpete Byrasandra lakes. ... with ₹43.14 crores worth of development works already underway.

projects.name: Sewage diversion channels, desilting, bund strengthening at lakes
projects.description: Construct diversion channels to block sewage entry; extensive desilting and bund strengthening; add scientific inlets/outlets, footpaths, security cabins, public toilets and integrate park development.
projects.verbatim_quote: To maintain water purity, we are constructing diversion channels to block sewage entry, alongside extensive desilting and bund strengthening.

projects.name: Ulsoor Lake comprehensive development (Brand Bengaluru + Minor Irrigation)
projects.description: Rs.82 crore comprehensive development project for Ulsoor Lake with Brand Bengaluru and Minor Irrigation Department; Rs.33 crore works in progress, next-phase tenders active; Rs.20 crore 2026-27 allocation for decorative grills and dedicated Kalyani.
projects.verbatim_quote: Specifically for the historic Ulsoor Lake, we have launched an ₹82 crore comprehensive development project in collaboration with the Brand Bengaluru scheme and the Minor Irrigation Department.

projects.name: Brand Bengaluru – Healthy Bengaluru: 7 maternity hospitals + 1 UCHC upgrade
projects.description: Upgrade 7 maternity hospitals into 30-bed FRU Community Health Centers and one hospital into a 50-bed FRU UCHC centre compliant with IPHS standards, with modular operation theatres, high-tech labs, specialist doctors.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Brand Bengaluru – Healthy Bengaluru initiative, we have allocated ₹40 crores to upgrade 7 maternity hospitals into 30-bed FRU Community Health Centers, and one hospital into a 50-bed FRU UCHC center, fully compliant with IPHS standards.

projects.name: Vatsalya Mane at Pobbatti Hospital
projects.description: Launch Vatsalya Mane at Pobbatti Hospital — a specialised day-care centre for comprehensive rehabilitation and development of specially-abled children.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will launch Vatsalya Mane at Pobbatti Hospital—a specialized day-care center dedicated to the comprehensive rehabilitation and development of specially-abled children.

projects.name: Indira Canteens (BCCC allocation)
projects.description: Rs.5 crore allocation to ensure continued operation of Indira Canteens providing affordable nutritious meals to daily-wage workers and students.
projects.verbatim_quote: We have allocated ₹5.00 crores to ensure they continue providing a dignified life for all.

projects.name: Bike Ambulance – First Responder Unit
projects.description: Bike Ambulance scheme (primarily CSR-funded) for Golden-Hour first response — nimble units providing immediate cost-free medical stabilisation.
projects.verbatim_quote: we proudly announce the Bike Ambulance – First Responder Unit scheme. Operating primarily through CSR funds, these nimble units will navigate traffic swiftly to provide immediate, cost-free medical stabilization and save lives during emergencies.

projects.name: Arogya Mitra – Smart Virtual Clinic (SVC) at 22 locations
projects.description: Rs.19 crore to launch Smart Virtual Clinic (SVC) across 22 locations under Arogya Mitra: Rs.8.90 crore equipment, Rs.5.07 crore two-year operations, Rs.5.03 crore 15-cabin centralised command centre.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Arogya Mitra initiative, we have allocated ₹19.00 crores to launch the Smart Virtual Clinic (SVC) scheme across 22 locations.

projects.name: Kirumalige – Shuddha Aahaara (Small Shop – Pure Food) pilot
projects.description: Rs.25 lakh pilot for food safety: FSSAI training and certification for street vendors; designated hygienic food zones in every ward.
projects.verbatim_quote: we are launching a pilot scheme named Kirumalige – Shuddha Aahaara (Small Shop – Pure Food) with a ₹25 lakh budget.

projects.name: Animal Husbandry Department total allocation
projects.description: Rs.5 crore overall for Animal Husbandry initiatives (balancing animal welfare with public safety).
projects.verbatim_quote: we have allocated ₹5.00 crores for revolutionary measures through our Animal Husbandry Department.

projects.name: Scientific shelters and rescue centres for street dogs
projects.description: Rs.2 crore for scientific shelters and specialised rescue centres to rehabilitate street dogs, adhering to Supreme Court directives, AWBI guidelines and PCA Act 1960.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will establish scientific shelters and specialized rescue centers for the rehabilitation of street dogs at a cost of ₹2.00 crores.

projects.name: New veterinary hospitals
projects.description: Rs.50 lakh to open new veterinary hospitals providing prompt free treatment to community dogs and pets of underprivileged families.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will open new veterinary hospitals at a cost of ₹50 lakhs to provide prompt, free treatment to community dogs and pets belonging to underprivileged families.

projects.name: Electric animal crematorium
projects.description: Rs.1 crore for BCCC's own electric animal crematorium for a dignified farewell for animals.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have allocated ₹1.00 crore to construct the Corporation's very own electric animal crematorium.

projects.name: Animal Birth Control (ABC) surgeries and mass vaccination
projects.description: Rs.1.44 crore for continuous ABC surgeries; Rs.80.94 lakh for mass disease-control vaccinations; Rs.60 lakh to repair Dandu ABC centre; nationwide-first combined vaccine for 15,216 street dogs; Rs.58.50 lakh to implant microchips in 30,432 dogs.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will execute continuous Animal Birth Control (ABC) surgeries at a cost of ₹1.44 crores and mass disease-control vaccinations at a cost of ₹80.94 lakhs.

projects.name: Parks maintenance and modernisation (215 parks)
projects.description: Rs.19 crore for park maintenance and Rs.15 crore for comprehensive modernisation/upgrades across the 215 parks in 2026-27; dedicated Putani Park (Children's Park) via CSR partnership.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have allocated ₹19 crores for park maintenance and ₹15 crores for comprehensive modernization and upgrades.

projects.name: Putani Park (Children's Park) via CSR
projects.description: Build a dedicated Putani Park (Children's Park) designed to foster physical and intellectual growth of the youngest citizens, partnering with CSR initiatives.
projects.verbatim_quote: Partnering with CSR initiatives, we will also build a dedicated Putani Park (Children's Park) designed specifically to foster the physical and intellectual growth of our youngest citizens.

projects.name: Flyover-underside beautification, bamboo saplings, Tree Parks
projects.description: Master plan to beautify spaces beneath flyovers with eco-friendly landscapes: plant bamboo saplings around roads and lakes; establish dedicated Tree Parks.
projects.verbatim_quote: we are rolling out a master plan to beautify the spaces beneath our flyovers. We are strongly emphasizing eco-friendly landscapes by planting bamboo saplings around roads and lakes, and by establishing dedicated Tree Parks.

projects.name: Namma Bengaluru – Namma Yojane (park / road-divider adoption)
projects.description: Expand Namma Bengaluru – Namma Yojane: encourage voluntary organisations and citizen groups to adopt parks and road dividers and cultivate ornamental plants.
projects.verbatim_quote: we are expanding the Namma Bengaluru – Namma Yojane (Our Bengaluru – Our Scheme) initiative.

projects.name: Tree canopy development + 50,000 roadside saplings
projects.description: Rs.11.50 crore for tree canopy development and Rs.3.5 crore to plant 50,000 roadside saplings; 25,000 nursery saplings for free public distribution; Rs.19.38 crore total urban forestry allocation including heritage-tree scientific care.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have allocated ₹11.50 crores for tree canopy development and ₹3.5 crores to plant 50,000 roadside saplings. ... We have set aside a total of ₹19.38 crores for these comprehensive initiatives.

projects.name: Individual housing for 500 beneficiaries (SC/ST/OBC/sanitation workers/specially-abled)
projects.description: Construct individual houses for 500 eligible beneficiaries at a total cost of Rs.35.70 crore: Rs.10 crore sanitation workers, Rs.17 crore SC/ST, Rs.5.50 crore OBC, Rs.3.20 crore specially-abled.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will construct individual houses for 500 eligible beneficiaries at a total cost of ₹35.70 crores.

projects.name: School fee reimbursement for SC/ST students
projects.description: Reimburse Rs.4.50 crore in school fees for SC/ST students.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will reimburse school fees amounting to ₹4.50 crores for SC/ST students.

projects.name: Scholarships for children of sanitation workers, gangmen and horticulture staff
projects.description: Rs.3 crore in scholarships for the education of children of sanitation workers, gangmen and horticulture staff.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will distribute ₹3.00 crores in scholarships to support the education of children of sanitation workers, gangmen, and horticulture staff.

projects.name: Free laptops to degree students
projects.description: Distribute free laptops to degree students at a cost of Rs.4 crore.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will distribute free laptops to degree students at a cost of ₹4.00 crores.

projects.name: Economically disadvantaged groups welfare
projects.description: Rs.1.50 crore allocation for welfare of economically disadvantaged groups.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have allocated ₹1.50 crores for the welfare of economically disadvantaged groups.

projects.name: Senior-citizen welfare programmes
projects.description: Rs.90 lakh for senior-citizen welfare programmes.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹90.00 lakhs specifically for senior citizen welfare programs.

projects.name: Upgrade of 16 shelter homes
projects.description: Rs.1.80 crore to upgrade 16 shelter homes within BCCC jurisdiction.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will also utilize ₹1.80 crores to upgrade 16 shelter homes within the Corporation's jurisdiction.

projects.name: Electric two-wheelers to SC/ST women entrepreneurs and sanitation workers + e-vending vehicles
projects.description: Rs.4 crore for electric two-wheelers for SC/ST women entrepreneurs and sanitation workers; e-vending vehicles for street vendors.
projects.verbatim_quote: At a total cost of ₹4.00 crores, we will provide electric two-wheelers to women entrepreneurs from SC/ST communities as well as to sanitation workers, along with e-vending vehicles for street vendors.

projects.name: 'Sarathi' scheme: 450 auto-rickshaws/cars for unemployed youth
projects.description: Rs.4.50 crore financial assistance to 450 unemployed youth to purchase auto-rickshaws and cars under Sarathi scheme.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the 'Sarathi' scheme, we will provide financial assistance of ₹4.50 crores to 450 unemployed youth to purchase auto-rickshaws and cars.

projects.name: Retro-fitted three-wheelers and electric wheelchairs for specially-abled
projects.description: Rs.3.50 crore to distribute retro-fitted three-wheelers and electric wheelchairs to specially-abled persons.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will distribute retro-fitted three-wheelers and electric wheelchairs to specially-abled persons at a cost of ₹3.50 crores.

projects.name: Women's empowerment: sewing machines and skill development training
projects.description: Rs.1.80 crore for sewing machines and skill-development training to drive women's empowerment.
projects.verbatim_quote: To drive women's empowerment, we have allocated ₹1.80 crores to provide sewing machines and organize skill development training.

projects.name: Musical instruments for sanitation workers' children
projects.description: Rs.1 crore to distribute musical instruments to identify and nurture artistic talents of sanitation workers' children.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will distribute musical instruments at a cost of ₹1.00 crore.

projects.name: 'Hiriyara Vadi' (Elders' Abode) residential complexes
projects.description: Construct free well-equipped residential complexes for senior citizens with no one to care for them, in partnership with voluntary organisations/CSR; BCCC provides land free, partnered NGOs manage facilities.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have formulated a noble new scheme called 'Hiriyara Vadi' (Elders' Abode). Under this, we will construct free, well-equipped residential complexes for senior citizens.

projects.name: Public Education total BCCC allocation
projects.description: Rs.6.85 crore set aside specifically for public education across BCCC's 92 educational institutions (50 kindergartens, 11 primary schools, 19 high schools, 9 PU colleges, 3 degree/PG colleges).
projects.verbatim_quote: We have set aside ₹6.85 crores specifically for public education.

projects.name: Free textbooks, notebooks, uniforms, bags, shoes, socks, sweaters
projects.description: Rs.4.50 crore for free textbooks, notebooks, uniforms, bags, shoes, socks and sweaters to students enrolling in BCCC schools and colleges.
projects.verbatim_quote: we have reserved ₹4.50 crores in the budget to provide free textbooks, notebooks, uniforms, bags, shoes, socks, and sweaters to students.

projects.name: SSLC/PUC morning-evening coaching + interactive digital boards
projects.description: Special morning and evening coaching for SSLC/PUC students; interactive digital boards in all schools and colleges; mandated syllabus completion by December.
projects.verbatim_quote: we are successfully conducting special morning and evening coaching classes, particularly for SSLC and PUC students. We have installed interactive digital boards in all schools and colleges.

projects.name: Career guidance programme for competitive exams
projects.description: Launch dedicated career-guidance programme with expert training to help students excel in competitive examinations.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will launch a dedicated career guidance program featuring special training from experts to help them excel in various competitive examinations.

projects.name: Free sports coaching (badminton, karate, cricket, swimming) + sports kits
projects.description: Professional coaching in badminton, karate, cricket and swimming through reputed institutions; free sports kits to talented athletes.
projects.verbatim_quote: We will provide professional coaching in sports such as badminton, karate, cricket, and swimming through reputed institutions. Furthermore, we will nurture talented athletes by distributing necessary sports kits completely free of cost.

projects.name: Music and theatre training + art supplies
projects.description: Specialised training in music and theatre; distribute essential art supplies to creative students.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will offer specialized training in music and theater, alongside distributing essential art supplies to creative students.

projects.name: School/college library upgrade and new institutions in new civic buildings
projects.description: Extensively upgrade libraries in Corporation schools and colleges; establish additional educational institutions in newly constructed civic buildings.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will extensively upgrade the libraries in Corporation schools and colleges, and we intend to establish additional educational institutions in newly constructed civic buildings.

projects.name: Hybrid pilot: physical education, yoga, mental wellness
projects.description: Launch hybrid-model pilot with trained instructors running weekly sessions on yoga, pranayama and meditation for exam-stress management; participants receive Corporation certification.
projects.verbatim_quote: We are launching an innovative, hybrid-model pilot program focusing on physical education, yoga, and mental wellness.

projects.name: AI training for students + 'Cyber Kavacha' ward workshops
projects.description: Introduce Artificial Intelligence training for BCCC students; launch 'Cyber Kavacha' awareness campaign with ward-level workshops to protect citizens from cyber fraud and online scams.
projects.verbatim_quote: we will introduce Artificial Intelligence (AI) training for our students. Furthermore, to protect our citizens in the digital age, we will launch the 'Cyber Kavacha' awareness campaign, organizing ward-level workshops.
